prerequisites: A RingCentral account with access to the RingCentral Developers portal (developers.ringcentral.com).
A registered REST API application in the RingCentral Developer Console, which provisions a Client ID and Client Secret used during authentication.
An authentication credential (a JWT credential or OAuth flow) configured for the app, used to obtain the access token supplied as auth_token.
The RingCentral account ID for account_id (or the tilde shorthand ~, which resolves to the account of the authenticated context).
The extension ID for extension_id identifying the specific user or department (or ~ to reference the extension of the authenticated context).

authMethods.name: Access token (Bearer token)
authMethods.summary: The auth_token field holds a RingCentral API access token used as a bearer token to authenticate REST API requests. RingCentral issues access tokens via its OAuth and OIDC token endpoint; for server-to-server integrations the token is typically obtained from a JWT credential, while interactive integrations use the 3-legged OAuth authorization code flow. The account_id and extension_id fields scope requests to a specific account and extension (each accepts the tilde ~ shorthand to use the authenticated context).

setupSteps.title: Register a REST API app and obtain credentials
setupSteps.body: Sign in to the RingCentral Developers portal and create a REST API application in the Developer Console. Provide the app details, choose an authentication method (JWT auth flow or 3-legged OAuth), and select the required scopes and access level. On creation, RingCentral provisions a Client ID and Client Secret that identify the app during authentication.

setupSteps.title: Generate an access token for auth_token
setupSteps.body: Create an authentication credential for the app. For server-to-server access, create a JWT credential and exchange it for an access token via RingCentral's OAuth token endpoint; for user-driven access, use the OAuth authorization code flow. Supply the resulting access token as the auth_token field.

setupSteps.title: Identify the account and extension
setupSteps.body: Set account_id to the target RingCentral account ID and extension_id to the target user or department extension ID. You may use the tilde (~) shorthand for either field to reference the account or extension associated with the authenticated context instead of an explicit numeric ID.

setupSteps.title: Provide all required fields
setupSteps.body: Configure the connector with account_id, auth_token, and extension_id. All three fields are required and are treated as secrets.
